findings.description: Rate limiting spec (Requirement: Semantic Scholar API Rate Limiting) says '~100 requests per 5 minutes shared pool' for unauthenticated access but provides no concrete implementation strategy. The spec says 'limits requests to stay within' but does not specify HOW — no token bucket, sliding window, or semaphore mechanism is specified. Without this, implementers may use a naive sleep-based approach that either over-throttles (wasting throughput) or under-throttles (hitting 429s). The design.md also only says 'retry logic (exponential backoff)' which is reactive, not proactive rate limiting.
findings.resolution: Add a requirement specifying a proactive rate limiter (e.g., asyncio.Semaphore or token bucket) with configurable RPS. Specify that the client SHALL track request timestamps and delay when approaching the limit, in addition to the reactive 429 backoff. Reference the existing httpx retry pattern used in other ingestion clients.

findings.description: Cross-source deduplication (Requirement: Scholar Paper Deduplication) proposes querying metadata_json for DOI/arXiv matches across ALL source types, but metadata_json is a JSONB column with no index. Scanning all Content records' metadata_json for DOI matches on every paper ingestion will be O(n) per paper — potentially very slow at scale. The spec does not address the query strategy or indexing.
findings.resolution: Either (a) add a GIN index on metadata_json for the doi and arxiv_id keys, (b) store DOI and arXiv ID as top-level nullable columns with unique indexes, or (c) specify that cross-source dedup only checks the last N days of content. Option (a) is lowest-risk since it doesn't require schema changes beyond an index. Add a requirement specifying the query strategy.

findings.description: The design references `canonical_id` in the cross-source deduplication scenario ('links the new record to the existing one via canonical_id if desired, or skips ingestion') but no `canonical_id` field exists on the Content model. This is an unresolved design choice that will confuse implementers — should they add a new column, or just skip duplicates?
findings.resolution: Remove the 'canonical_id' reference and specify the concrete behavior: skip ingestion and log a debug message when a cross-source duplicate is detected. If canonical linking is desired in the future, it should be a separate proposal.

findings.description: The spec has no error handling scenarios for Semantic Scholar API failures beyond 429 rate limiting. The API can return 404 (paper not found), 400 (invalid paper ID format), 500 (server error), and network timeouts. The single-paper lookup scenario ('resolves the identifier to a Semantic Scholar paper ID') does not specify what happens when resolution fails — should it raise, log and skip, or return a partial result?
findings.resolution: Add error handling scenarios: (1) 404 on paper lookup → log warning, skip paper, (2) invalid identifier format → raise ValueError with helpful message, (3) 5xx/timeout → retry with backoff up to 3 attempts then skip with warning, (4) batch endpoint partial failures → ingest successful papers, log failures. Follow the existing fail-safe pattern from RSS/podcast ingestion.

findings.description: No contracts directory exists for this proposal. The design specifies Pydantic response models (S2Paper, S2Author, S2SearchResult) and result dataclasses (ScholarSearchResult, ScholarPaperResult) but these are not formalized as contracts. For a /parallel-plan-feature proposal, contracts and work-packages.yaml are expected but missing — this limits parallelizability.
findings.resolution: This proposal was generated by a non-parallel plan skill, so missing contracts/work-packages is expected. For implementation, either (a) generate contracts and work-packages before dispatching to /parallel-implement-feature, or (b) implement linearly with /implement-feature. Not blocking, but note the gap.

findings.description: The design specifies ScholarWebSearchProvider registered in get_web_search_provider() factory, but the existing factory uses a settings-based default (settings.web_search_provider). The spec doesn't clarify whether 'scholar' can be set as the default provider or is only accessible via explicit provider='scholar' parameter. Setting it as default would break the general web search functionality since Semantic Scholar only indexes academic papers.
findings.resolution: Clarify that 'scholar' is an opt-in provider only available via explicit provider='scholar' parameter, never as the default WEB_SEARCH_PROVIDER. Add a note in the requirement that the factory should accept it but the settings validation should not list it as a valid default.

findings.description: The source configuration design shows `year_range: '2024-'` as a string but the Semantic Scholar API accepts `year` as a query parameter with format 'YYYY-YYYY' or 'YYYY-'. The parsing of this string into API parameters is not specified — implementers need to know whether to pass it directly or parse it into start/end years.
findings.resolution: Add a note in the design that year_range is passed directly to the S2 API 'year' parameter (the API accepts both '2024-' and '2023-2024' natively). Alternatively, split into year_min/year_max integer fields for clarity.

findings.description: The SEMANTIC_SCHOLAR_API_KEY is described as optional with empty default. The design correctly uses x-api-key header, which is fine. However, the key should be wired through the secrets system (.secrets.yaml) rather than plain .env to follow the established pattern.
findings.resolution: Task 3.6 already covers base.yaml wiring with ${SEMANTIC_SCHOLAR_API_KEY:-}. Ensure .secrets.yaml documentation mentions this key. No code change needed.

findings.description: Reference extraction (task 5.6) uses the batch API for up to 500 papers per request, but if a large content corpus references hundreds of unique papers, the extraction + resolution could be slow. The spec does not specify pagination or progress reporting for the reference extraction workflow.
findings.resolution: Add a --limit flag to scholar-refs CLI to cap the number of papers resolved per run. Add progress logging (e.g., 'Resolved 50/200 references...'). The batch API already handles up to 500, so pagination within a single batch call is not needed.

findings.description: Task 9.1 says 'Update src/api/source_routes.py to include scholar sources in SourcesOverview' but the frontend ingest page (just merged as PR #330) already renders source types from a sourceConfig map. The frontend will show 'Other' for scholar content unless the sourceConfig is also updated.
findings.resolution: Add a task to update web/src/routes/contents.tsx sourceConfig (and the new ingest.tsx) to include a 'Scholar' entry with an appropriate icon (e.g., GraduationCap from lucide-react).
